summary.brief: Barton v. Advanced Radiology. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: A jury found that appellees breached the standard of care in the treatment of Lana Burton and that this breach was a cause of her death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Burton underwent a routine breast cancer examination at Advanced Radiology. The results of that examination indicated she had no abnormalities.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Burton found a lump in her right breast and returned to Advanced Radiology. They performed a mammogram and an ultrasound examination.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A mammogram and ultrasound showed abnormalities that were 'highly suspicious for extensive malignancy in the right breast centrally and in the lower outer quadrant [of the right breast] with malignant adenopathy.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Burton underwent a biopsy that revealed that she did, in fact, have Stage III, 'triple negative' breast cancer.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Zuurbier noted that Dr. Minkin did not perform a biopsy of the lump. He only performed a mammogram and an ultrasound.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Dr. Pushkas opined that she had Stage I cancer in May 11, 2012. But, eighteen months later, when she returned to Advanced Radiology on August 9, 2013, the tumor had grown, and she was diagnosed with 'triple negative' Stage III breast cancer.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: The trial court abused its discretion in granting appellees’ motion JNOV.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We reverse the trial court’s entry of judgment in appellees’ favor and reinstate the jury’s verdict and award.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We conclude that the jury could have reasonably found that if Dr. Minkin had caught Ms. Burton’s cancer in May 2012, she had an 80% probability of not dying from Stage I cancer.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient found a lump in her right breast and returned to Advanced Radiology for evaluation.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Breast lump - differential includes benign mass vs. malignancy

reveal.result_summary: Abnormalities highly suspicious for extensive malignancy
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt biopsy to confirm diagnosis

reveal.result_summary: Abnormalities highly suspicious for extensive malignancy
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt biopsy to confirm diagnosis

reveal.clinical_significance: Confirms diagnosis of aggressive breast cancer

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with a breast lump

key_events.event: Patient reports lump in right breast

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Imaging and biopsy to diagnose breast lump

key_events.event: Biopsy performed revealing Stage III cancer

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presents with a breast lump
decision_points.prompt: What diagnostic procedure should be performed to confirm the nature of the breast lump?

options.description: Perform a biopsy of the breast lump
options.clinical_reasoning: Biopsy is the definitive test to diagnose breast cancer

options.description: Rely on mammogram and ultrasound findings only
options.clinical_reasoning: Mammogram and ultrasound are not definitive without biopsy

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not perform a biopsy

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established biopsy is standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to perform biopsy led to delayed diagnosis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Biopsy would have confirmed cancer earlier

patient_outcome.description: Cancer spread to liver, lungs, and lymph nodes
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Patient died from metastatic breast cancer

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to perform biopsy
Causation established - earlier diagnosis would have prevented metastasis

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Biopsy would have shown cancer earlier

factual_timeline.event: Patient reports lump in right breast

complications.complication: Metastasis to liver, lungs, and lymph nodes
